summary:
In mandibular incisors, an occurrence rate between 20-41% for having two canals exists based on studies showing typical single-rooted structure; however, some variations are noted making it relatively uncommon yet not entirely absent from dental practice observations and literature reviews conducted. To summarize further while adhering strictly per instruction provided:

In mandibular incisors, approximately one-fifth to nearly half exhibit dual-canal configurations due primarily to individual anatomical variation though predominantly featuring singular internal structures as reported across multiple research sources consulted herein.
